config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak individu adalah batas yang mencegah kekuasaan mayoritas bertindak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menginjak martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menomorsatukan satu pihak secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, the whole point of rights is to put hard limits on what majorities and governments can do, because “the common good” has often been used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require real limits, but those limits should be narrow and justified. So I would frame it as a balance where individual rights are the default, and society has to earn any exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau keputusan yang merugikan orang kecil, jadi hak individu memang perlu jadi pagar yang tegas. Namun, saya juga tidak setuju kalau hak individu dipahami seolah-olah bebas tanpa batas; dalam isu seperti kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan ketertiban, pembatasan bisa sah asal jelas, proporsional, dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Jadi buat saya, yang benar bukan menomorsatukan masyarakat secara mutlak, melainkan menempatkan hak individu sebagai dasar, lalu kepentingan bersama hanya boleh membatasi secara ketat dan terukur.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: From my U.S. perspective, I still reject the idea that society should generally outrank the individual, because that logic has too often been used here to justify trampling unpopular minorities and expanding government power beyond what is necessary. At the same time, I do not buy an absolutist view of individual freedom either, because public health, safety, and functioning institutions sometimes require limited restraints that protect everyone. The key issue is not whether society matters, but whether any restriction is narrowly tailored, evidence-based, and accountable rather than a vague appeal to the “common good.” So I would put individual rights first as the default, with society allowed to override them only in exceptional cases and under strict limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi pembenaran untuk menekan yang lemah, jadi hak individu memang harus jadi pagar utama terhadap kekuasaan mayoritas maupun negara. Tapi saya juga tidak melihat hak individu sebagai sesuatu yang mutlak tanpa batas; pembatasan bisa sah kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, misalnya untuk kesehatan publik atau keselamatan. Jadi, bagi saya, yang paling masuk akal adalah menempatkan hak individu sebagai dasar, lalu kepentingan bersama hanya boleh membatasi dalam keadaan khusus, bukan sebagai prinsip utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From a U.S. perspective, that kind of claim has too often been a cover for majority rule, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against people who are unpopular or politically weak. I do think there are real exceptions for public health, safety, and basic order, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable rather than justified by a vague appeal to the common good. So my view is that individual rights should be the default, and society should only override them in clearly exceptional cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
